Details:
There are a number of commercially available products employing reverse osmosis for control of strontium in drinking water.  Four were tested in USEPA's Environmental Technology Verification project.  Natural strontium was effectively removed (97 to greater than 99%) [15] [16] [617] [1015]. Reverse osmosis is a Best Available Technology for the control of beta particle emitters like radioactive strontium. 
Another study using selective complexation combined with a nanofiltration membrane achieved 100% removal [2185]. Effects of transmembrane pressure, pH, ligand concentration and ionic strength on salts retention are described, and the complexation constants are estimated. Strontium retentions are nearly constant with increasing transmembrane pressure. Above pH 6, strontium retention increases sharply (90% to 100% retention) [2185]. Strontium retention increases with poly(acrylic acid) concentration (76% to 98% retention) [2185]. Experiments showed high sodium salt retention involves a decline in filtrations fluxes, large water addition to stabilize retentate salinity during concentrations, and a decrease in strontium retention (100% to 65% retention) [2185].
